sense.definition: a Japanese martial art that focuses on redirecting an opponent's energy rather than meeting force with force
a premier mixed martial arts organization known for its high-level competitions featuring fighters from various disciplines

examples.x: He dreams of becoming a champion in the Ultimate Fighting Championship one day.
She earned her spot in the Ultimate Fighting Championship after years of training.

sense.definition: any type of sports that include fighting which are especially originated in the Far East, such as judo, kung fu, etc.

examples.x: He has been studying martial arts since he was a child, starting with karate and later branching out into jiu-jitsu.
Many martial arts emphasize both physical training and mental discipline.
The dojo offers classes in a wide range of martial arts, including taekwondo, kung fu, and judo.
Practicing martial arts has helped her improve her focus and self-confidence.
Martial arts tournaments attract competitors from around the world to showcase their skills and techniques.

sense.definition: a Chinese martial art encompassing a wide range of fighting styles and techniques

examples.x: He practiced kung fu for years to master its intricate techniques.
The instructor demonstrated a powerful kung fu kick during the class.
They studied different kung fu styles, from Shaolin to Wing Chun.

sense.definition: a Korean martial art characterized by its emphasis on high kicks and jumping and spinning kicks
a martial art and sport that emphasizes grappling and throwing techniques,  originated in Japan
a Japanese martial art and combat sport that focuses on ground fighting and grappling techniques

examples.x: He trained in jiujitsu to improve his self-defense skills.
She earned her black belt in jiujitsu after years of dedicated practice.
The school offers classes for beginners and advanced students in jiujitsu.

sense.definition: a martial art that involves striking and blocking techniques, typically practiced for self-defense, sport, or physical fitness

examples.x: She practiced karate for years before earning her black belt.
In karate, students learn both techniques and forms.
She teaches karate at the local dojo every Saturday.

sense.definition: a Japanese martial art that focuses on swordsmanship with bamboo swords and protective armor

examples.x: He has been practicing kendo for over a decade.
The dojo offers classes in kendo for all skill levels.
She competed in a regional kendo tournament last month.
The kendo practitioner wore traditional protective armor during practice.
They traveled to Japan to participate in a kendo seminar.

sense.definition: a Brazilian martial art that combines elements of dance, acrobatics, and music

examples.x: She has been practicing capoeira for years and has mastered its fluid movements.
He demonstrated his agility and strength through a series of capoeira kicks.
Learning capoeira has taught me to be more flexible and coordinated.

sense.definition: a Chinese full-contact fighting system incorporating punches, kicks, throws, and sweeps
a combat sport that focuses on grappling and ground fighting techniques
a traditional form of Thai martial arts characterized by its emphasis on practical fighting techniques

examples.x: She traveled to Thailand to study Muay Boran from experienced masters.
The history of Muay Boran dates back centuries in Thai culture.
Muay Boran focuses on utilizing the entire body as a weapon in combat.
Muay Boran incorporates a wide range of strikes, throws, and grappling techniques.
The Muay Boran fighters showcased their skills in a traditional tournament.

sense.definition: a Korean martial art that focuses on joint locks, throws, and strikes for self-defense

examples.x: She practiced hapkido diligently to improve her self-defense skills.
He earned his black belt in hapkido after years of training.
The hapkido instructor demonstrated a variety of striking techniques.
The principles of hapkido teach practitioners to remain calm and focused during confrontations.
She used her hapkido training to effectively defend herself in a real-life situation.

sense.definition: a martial art that originated in Russia, emphasizing grappling techniques and self-defense

examples.x: The Russian army used sambo as part of their hand-to-hand combat training.
Learning sambo helped him become more proficient in ground fighting.
Many UFC fighters have backgrounds in sambo, showcasing its effectiveness in mixed martial arts competitions.
He trained in sambo to improve his grappling skills for mixed martial arts.

sense.definition: a sport in which fighters wear special gloves and use only their fists to hit each other
the adapted version of taekwondo for athletes with physical disabilities, allowing them to participate in the sport

examples.x: She excelled in para taekwondo competitions despite her mobility impairment.
The para taekwondo athlete demonstrated incredible skill and determination in the ring.
The coach tailored training sessions to meet the specific needs of para taekwondo athletes.
She overcame numerous challenges to become a para taekwondo champion.

sense.definition: the practice of judo adapted for athletes with physical disabilities, emphasizing techniques suitable for their abilities and circumstances
a martial art in which two people fight using long and thin swords

examples.x: Fencing requires agility and precision to outmaneuver an opponent.
He joined a fencing club to learn how to wield a foil.
The fencing match was intense, with both fencers showcasing their skills.
She has been practicing fencing since she was a child and competes in tournaments.
The school offers fencing as an extracurricular activity for students interested in the sport.

sense.definition: a combar sport where individuals with physical disabilities engage in fencing while seated in a wheelchair

examples.x: Wheelchair fencing allows individuals with physical disabilities to compete in the sport of fencing.
She trained tirelessly to excel in wheelchair fencing at the international level.
The Paralympic Games feature wheelchair fencing as one of its events.
He discovered his passion for wheelchair fencing after trying out different adaptive sports.
Wheelchair fencing requires both precision and agility from its athletes.

sense.definition: the skill or art of using a sword, typically in combat or as a sport

examples.x: He practiced swordplay with a fencing coach to prepare for tournaments.
Her swordplay technique improved after years of dedicated training.
Swordplay requires precision, speed, and strategic thinking.
They studied historical swordplay techniques from different cultures.
Swordplay competitions showcase athletes' skill and agility with blades.

sense.definition: a defensive technique in fencing where a fighter deflects or blocks an opponent's attack with their weapon

examples.x: The swordsman's parry set up a successful counterattack.
They studied different parry techniques in martial arts class.
The fencer practiced parries to improve their defensive skills.
During the intense bout, she relied on her footwork and parries to maintain distance and control.
She used a circular parry to deflect the opponent's blade away from her target.

sense.definition: a form of mixed martial arts competition held within an enclosed, caged area

examples.x: Cage fighting matches are intense and brutal.
He trained for years to compete in cage fighting.
Many people watch cage fighting for its excitement.
She is one of the top athletes in cage fighting.
Cage fighting combines techniques from various martial arts.

sense.definition: a sport in which two players hold each other while trying to throw or force the other one to the ground
a form of combat sport where opponents grapple and attempt to pin each other to the mat using various techniques, emphasizing takedowns and ground control

examples.x: He began training in freestyle wrestling at a young age.
The Olympic gold medalist excelled in freestyle wrestling.
She competed in freestyle wrestling tournaments across the country.
Freestyle wrestling requires a combination of strength and technique.
His favorite aspect of freestyle wrestling is executing takedowns.

sense.definition: a style of wrestling where holds below the waist are prohibited, emphasizing throws and takedowns
a wrestling technique used to gain control over an opponent by taking them down to the mat or ground

examples.x: They studied different setups for executing effective takedowns.
Takedowns are essential for controlling the pace and outcome of a wrestling match.
His quick takedown surprised his opponent and earned him the advantage.
She used a takedown to gain dominant position on the mat.

sense.definition: a technique used by a wrestler to escape from a disadvantaged position and gain control over their opponent
a technique used by a wrestler to reverse their opponent's position from the bottom to the top
a maneuver used by a wrestler to break free from their opponent's hold or pinning attempt
a situation that occurs when one wrestler holds both of the opponent's shoulders on the mat simultaneously for a specified period

examples.x: The referee counted to three, signaling a pin and victory for the wrestler.
He celebrated his pin with teammates and coaches after the match.
The wrestler's pin technique improved significantly over the season.
She earned points for near falls leading up to the pin.

sense.definition: a situation in wrestling when a wrestler almost pins their opponent, achieving a count of two but not the full three required for a pin

examples.x: The wrestler scored a near fall but couldn't get the pin.
His near fall earned him two points.
A near fall can change the momentum of a match.
The referee counted a near fall during the match.
A near fall shows you're close to winning.

sense.definition: the defensive stance and positioning that athletes adopt to protect themselves from attacks while maintaining readiness to counter in combat sports like boxing and fencing

examples.x: The boxer lowered his guard momentarily, leaving an opening for a counterpunch.
He kept a tight guard throughout the match to defend against his opponent's jabs.
She adopted a strong guard to counter her opponent's attacks effectively.
The fencer adjusted her guard to adapt to her opponent's tactics.
His wide guard helped him block and counter his opponent's attacks effectively.

sense.definition: a wrestling move where a wrestler jumps and kicks their opponent while in mid-air

examples.x: She surprised her opponent with a sudden dropkick.
The wrestler dodged the dropkick with a quick move.
She aimed for her opponent's chest with the dropkick.
The dropkick caught his opponent off guard.
His signature move was the flying dropkick.

sense.definition: (professional wrestling) the situation when a good character switches to a bad character

examples.x: The wrestler's heel turn shocked the audience.
His heel turn happened when he attacked his tag team partner.
The audience couldn't believe his sudden heel turn.
Her heel turn was marked by an ambush on her former mentor.
After his heel turn, he cheated to win every match.

sense.definition: (combat sports) a kicking technique where the leg is thrust outward from the body's side, typically targeting an opponent's midsection or legs

examples.x: He landed a powerful sidekick to his opponent's ribs.
Her sidekick technique knocked her opponent off balance.
The fighter's sidekick landed with precision, scoring a knockdown.
He countered his opponent's attack with a swift sidekick.
The fighter's signature move is the spinning sidekick.

sense.definition: a technique in martial arts where a fighter thrusts their foot forward to create distance

examples.x: The instructor demonstrated the proper form for a push kick.
He used a push kick to stop the opponent's advance.
Her push kick helped control the pace of the match.
She surprised him with a quick push kick.
The push kick knocked his opponent off balance.

sense.definition: a combat sport that blends striking and grappling techniques from various disciplines, both standing and on the ground

examples.x: She dedicated years to mastering the techniques of mixed martial arts.
The event featured some of the best athletes in mixed martial arts from around the world.
His journey in mixed martial arts began with a passion for martial arts films.
The rise in popularity of mixed martial arts has led to increased interest in combat sports globally.
He trained in various martial arts disciplines to excel in mixed martial arts.

sense.definition: a submission technique in combat sports where pressure is applied to the opponent's ankle joint by hooking their heel

examples.x: He executed a flawless heel hook from the guard position.
The heel hook is known for its effectiveness in ending fights quickly.
She used a heel hook to immobilize her opponent and force a tap out.
The fighter expertly countered his opponent's takedown attempt with a heel hook.
The heel hook is considered one of the most dangerous submissions in MMA.

sense.definition: (combat sports) the imaginary line down the middle of a fighter's body

examples.x: He struck down the centerline with a jab.
Protect your centerline with a strong guard.
She kicked straight down his centerline.
The boxer aimed punches along the centerline.
The fighter targeted the opponent's centerline.

sense.definition: a weight class in combat sports, typically including competitors weighing up to 51 kg
a weight class in combat sports, typically including boxers or mixed martial artists weighing between 52.2 and 53.5 kg

examples.x: The bantamweight bout was the highlight of the evening.
Bantamweight matches often feature high-intensity exchanges.
The bantamweight rankings were updated after the recent tournament.

sense.definition: a weight class in combat sports for fighters who weigh between 56 and 57 kg

examples.x: He transitioned from lightweight to featherweight to improve his chances.

sense.definition: (in boxing) a weight usually between 59 and 61.2 kilograms
a weight between lightweight and middleweight in boxing and other sports, usually between 63 and 67 kilograms

examples.x: The welterweight division features athletes who compete in boxing and other sports, with participants weighing between the lightweight and middleweight categories.
He aspired to join the ranks of welterweight champions, dedicating himself to rigorous training and conditioning to excel in his chosen sport.
The welterweight title fight drew attention from fans worldwide, as two formidable contenders prepared to battle for supremacy in the ring.
She made history by becoming the first female welterweight champion in mixed martial arts, inspiring a new generation of athletes to pursue their dreams.
The welterweight competition at the Olympics showcased the talent and skill of athletes from diverse backgrounds, competing for medals and glory on the world stage.

sense.definition: (in wrestling and boxing) a weight in the heaviest category which is above 91kg
a weight class in boxing and other combat sports, typically for competitors weighing between 70 and 73 kg

examples.x: He won the middleweight championship last year.
She trained hard to compete in the middleweight division.
The middleweight title fight was the highlight of the night.
He moved up from welterweight to middleweight this season.

sense.definition: a weight class in boxing and mixed martial arts for competitors who weigh between 76 and 79 kg

examples.x: The light heavyweight belt is one of the most coveted in the sport.
Several light heavyweight contenders are vying for the title.
The light heavyweight match was the highlight of the evening.
His reach gives him an advantage in the light heavyweight class.

sense.definition: a weight class in boxing or wrestling for competitors who weigh between 85 and 90.7 kg

examples.x: She feels stronger and faster at the cruiserweight class.
The upcoming fight will feature two top cruiserweight contenders.
The new cruiserweight rules have increased the weight limit to 91 kg.
He had to lose 5 kg to qualify for the cruiserweight bout.
The boxer moved up to the cruiserweight division after winning the light heavyweight title.

sense.definition: a weight class in combat sports, typically for competitors weighing over a specific limit, often above 91 kg

examples.x: She broke the record in the super heavyweight lifting competition.
The super heavyweight event drew a large crowd at the arena.
The super heavyweight fighters are known for their immense power.
His training focused on preparing for the super heavyweight bout.
At 110 kg, he easily qualified for the super heavyweight class.

sense.definition: a Japanese combat sport where two wrestlers aim to force each other out of a ring or to the ground

examples.x: He trained rigorously to compete in sumo wrestling tournaments.
The objective of sumo wrestling is to push or throw your opponent out of the ring.
Sumo wrestling matches are steeped in ritual and tradition.
She admired the discipline and technique of sumo wrestling.
Sumo wrestling bouts are often brief but intense.

sense.definition: a sporting match in which individuals wrestle

examples.x: Spectators eagerly awaited the highly anticipated bout between the two undefeated wrestlers.
The annual wrestling bout drew competitors from across the country to showcase their skills.
He trained rigorously for weeks to prepare for his upcoming bout in the wrestling tournament.
In the final bout of the evening, two seasoned wrestlers faced off in a thrilling display of athleticism.
Despite his injury, he refused to withdraw from the bout, determined to prove himself on the wrestling mat.

sense.definition: a form of martial arts known for its practical self-defense techniques

examples.x: I've been practicing Krav Maga for three years now.
She teaches Krav Maga classes at the local gym.
Krav Maga focuses on real-world scenarios and quick reactions.
Many police and military units around the world use Krav Maga techniques.
She earned a black belt in Krav Maga after years of dedicated training.

sense.definition: an ancient Greek martial art combining boxing and wrestling techniques

examples.x: Pankration was a featured sport in the ancient Olympic Games.
Competitors in pankration aimed to submit their opponents by any means necessary.
Historians believe pankration originated in ancient Sparta and Athens.
Pankration matches could be brutal and often ended with one fighter yielding.
Greek sculptures depict athletes engaged in pankration bouts.

sense.definition: (in professional wrestling) the act of touching a teammate to switch places in a match

examples.x: The tag was perfectly timed to change the match’s momentum.
The wrestler made a quick tag to bring in his partner.
After the tag, the new wrestler entered the ring with energy.
He stretched his arm for the tag while avoiding his opponent.

sense.definition: (of wrestlers or boxers) to let go of or come out of a grappling position, particularly when asked by the referee

examples.x: It's important for wrestlers to know when to break to avoid penalties.
The crowd cheered as the fighters finally broke.
The boxer clinched, and the referee told them to break.
